mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, labor market weakness signals severe demand contraction, causing S&P 500 forward returns to turn negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5%, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment stays below 7%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and consumer prices indicate demand-pull inflation that forces monetary tightening expectations, driving S&P 500 forward returns negative over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL growth remains below 2% annually, or interaction term Rank IC is negative over 24 months.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its 12-month moving average signals supply-side recovery that boosts short-term equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO outperformance fails to generate positive 5-day returns during financial stress periods, or the signal reverses direction when market volatility exceeds historical averages.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding economic activity and consumer spending capacity, which consistently supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple market cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly inflation spikes trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally following CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.5% MoM, or the negative return pattern disappears when inflation remains above 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in housing starts and real GDP growth confirms broad-based demand recovery, driving sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns when housing starts rise while GDP growth stagnates, or the interaction effect vanishes in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2% annually confirms economic expansion, which reliably generates positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days during normal growth phases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above 2% fails to predict positive 5-day returns during liquidity crunches, or the signal consistently underperforms random walk benchmarks.
